question: Verify the abbreviations related to the NRC and explain NuScale's NRC licensing approach through the text.

nuscale_ch01_0117.title: Figure 1.7-3f: Piping and In strumentation Diagram Legends
nuscale_ch01_0117.summary: This section provides the legend for piping and instrumentation diagrams (P&IDs) used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report. It lists and describes various symbols representing sample probes, pressure and temperature devices, flow measurement instruments, level sensors, and other auxiliary and accessory devices used in the diagrams. These legends ensure consistent interpretation of P&IDs throughout the documentation.

nuscale_ch01_0076.title: Conformance with Standard Review Plan
nuscale_ch01_0076.summary: NuScale reviewed the Standard Review Plan (SRP), including relevant guidance and acceptance criteria, and documented any gaps in a summary report submitted to the NRC. The company also assessed conformance with the Design-Specific Review Standard (DSRS) for small modular reactors and addressed applicable Three Mile Island (TMI) and generic safety issues as required by regulations, with details provided in referenced tables and FSAR sections.

nuscale_ch01_0111.title: Figure 1.7-2: Instrumentatio n and Controls Symbol Legend
nuscale_ch01_0111.summary: This section provides a legend explaining the symbols used for instrumentation and controls in NuScale’s safety analysis diagrams. It defines the logic gate functions (AND, OR, NOT), bistable outputs, control room indicators, switch types, and other control functions, ensuring clear interpretation of system diagrams and operational statuses. Additionally, it includes notes on piping and instrumentation diagram conventions, such as vent and drain representations.

nuscale_ch01_0858.title: SRP 19.2, Initial Issuance: Review of Risk Information Used to Support Permanent PlantSpecific Changes to the Licensing Basis: General GuidanceAll Various
nuscale_ch01_0858.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with various sections of the Standard Review Plan (SRP) related to risk information and safety requirements for licensing basis changes. It notes that SRP 19.2 is not applicable to NuScale but applies to licensees proposing plant-specific changes, while NuScale conforms or partially conforms to other SRP sections, with exceptions only where criteria are not relevant to its design (such as below-grade spent fuel pools). The table provides a structured overview of these conformance determinations.

nuscale_ch01_0129.title: 1.9.4 Operational Experience (Generic Communications)
nuscale_ch01_0129.summary: NuScale reviewed the Standard Review Plan (SRP) and related guidance to ensure their design meets NRC regulatory criteria, documenting any gaps and their resolutions. They also assessed conformance with requirements from the Three Mile Island incident and addressed unresolved and generic safety issues as required by NRC regulations, referencing relevant reports and guidance. This information is summarized in specific tables and sections of their Final Safety Analysis Report.

nuscale_ch01_0859.title: SRP 19.3, Rev 0: Regulatory Treatment of Non-Safety Systems for Passive Advanced Light Water ReactorsAll Various
nuscale_ch01_0859.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with SRP 19.3, Rev 0, which addresses the regulatory treatment of non-safety systems for passive advanced light water reactors. The document states that NuScale fully conforms to the requirements of SRP 19.3, with no exceptions or additional comments noted.

nuscale_ch01_0130.title: 1.9.5 Advanced and Evolutionary Light-Water Reactor Design Issues
nuscale_ch01_0130.summary: This section describes how the NuScale design conforms to regulatory criteria by incorporating relevant operational experience and NRC guidance, including bulletins, generic letters, and 10 CFR 21 notifications, into its safety analysis and supplier evaluation processes. It also addresses advanced and evolutionary light-water reactor design issues, following NRC policy and technical guidance as outlined in SECY-93-087 and related documents.

nuscale_ch01_0013.title: Proprietary Information
nuscale_ch01_0013.summary: This section explains the organization and identification of text, tables, figures, and page numbering within the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR). It also clarifies that while the FSAR does not contain proprietary or safeguards information, some sensitive content is withheld from public disclosure in accordance with regulations, and a separate public version is provided with such material removed.

nuscale_ch01_0014.title: Acronyms and Abbreviations
nuscale_ch01_0014.summary: This section explains the conventions used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR) for numbering tables, figures, and pages, as well as the handling of proprietary and sensitive information. It clarifies that sensitive material is withheld from the public version in accordance with regulatory requirements, while all such content is clearly marked in the non-public version.

nuscale_ch01_0079.title: Advanced and Evolutionary Light-Water Reactor Design Issues
nuscale_ch01_0079.summary: This section explains that the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report aligns with regulatory criteria by incorporating relevant operational experience and NRC guidance, including bulletins and generic letters, into the design. It also details the evaluation of supplier compliance with 10 CFR 21 notifications and confirms that all applicable notifications have been addressed, ensuring the design meets advanced and evolutionary light-water reactor licensing and policy issues as outlined by the NRC.
